title: [APIv2] push: unauthorized: authentication required
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I was using APIv2 to push an image to dockerhub, but got a 400. I think the steps are correct, if they are wrong please correct me.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. set up APIv2 service: `podman system service --time 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8081 &> ~/server.log &`

2.     podman tag alpine docker.io/edwardf0cus/testrepo:latest

3.     XRA=`echo "{ \"username\": \"edwardf0cus\", \"password\": \"mypassword\", \"email\": \"myemail@website.com\", \"serveraddress\":\"docker.io\"}" | base64 --wrap=0`

4.     curl -v -X POST -H "X-Registry-Auth: $XRA" "http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/images/docker.io/edwardf0cus/testrepo/push?tag=latest"

**Describe the results you received:**
```
# curl -v -X POST -H "X-Registry-Auth: $XRA" "http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/images/docker.io/edwardf0cus/testrepo/push?tag=latest"
*   Trying ::1:8081...
* connect to ::1 port 8081 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8081...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8081 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/images/docker.io/edwardf0cus/testrepo/push?tag=latest HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8081
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> X-Registry-Auth: eyAidXNlcm5hbWUiOiAiZWR3YXJkZjBjdXMiLCAicGFzc3dvcmQiOiAiamhfMDI2MTM3IiwgImVtYWlsIjogInNoZW53ZWk2MDRAMTI2LmNvbSIsICJzZXJ2ZXJhZGRyZXNzIjoiZG9ja2VyLmlvIn0K
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Tue, 26 May 2020 08:33:32 GMT
< Content-Length: 462
<
{"cause":"errors:\ndenied: requested access to the resource is denied\nunauthorized: authentication required\n","message":"Error pushing image \"docker.io/edwardf0cus/testrepo\": Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Error initiating layer upload to /v2/edwardf0cus/testrepo/blobs/uploads/ in registry-1.docker.io: errors:\ndenied: requested access to the resource is denied\nunauthorized: authentication required\n","response":400}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
push successfully and have a correct 200 code returned.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.0.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0-dev
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.15-1.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.15, commit: 33da5ef83bf2abc7965fc37980a49d02fdb71826'
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "32"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.6.6-300.fc32.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 787238912
  memTotal: 2068803584
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 2203840512
  swapTotal: 2206199808
  uptime: 25h 16m 7.11s (Approximately 1.04 days)
registries:
  localhost:
    Blocked: false
    Insecure: true
    Location: localhost
    MirrorByDigestOnly: false
    Mirrors: []
    Prefix: localhost
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com

  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0
    running: 1
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 2
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 1590475861

  GitCommit: 1077d2d0b72d027a01080c0d3ebdb7d99a969661

  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.0.0-dev
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the bug, @Edward5hen!

https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6207 will add support for authentication.
